Worldwide race to trace passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship

By a BBC reporter

Health authorities across three continents are scrambling to trace passengers from a cruise ship that docked last week, after a confirmed case of hantavirus pulmonary syndrome among the crew. The ship had visited ports in four countries during its three-week voyage.

Contact tracing

Officials in the Canaries say tracing efforts have been complicated by the fact that several passengers disembarked before symptoms appeared. The incubation period for hantavirus can extend up to five weeks, making retrospective tracing difficult.

What is hantavirus?

Hantavirus is a family of viruses spread mainly by rodents. Pulmonary syndrome is the most severe form and can be fatal. Health agencies advise avoiding contact with rodents and their droppings, particularly in enclosed spaces.
